forbidden_changes: do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not construct success_like preference pairs; no high-confidence success_like trajectories
do not treat partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: restructure approach_progress to strongly penalize early_failure trajectories (e.g., negative baseline or quality-based scaling that yields negative reward for low progress or high speed/angle)

replace terminal_reward with separate crash_penalty (sufficiently negative, e.g., -10 or more) for unsafe terminations and safe_landing_bonus (positive under stringent conditions: both legs, very low speed, small angle, sustained leg contact)
preserve progress-conditioned gating (far/near stages) and the positive-favoring components: proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus, low_progress_timeout
ensure new crash_penalty and safe_landing_bonus are gated by stage (e.g., near_stage) to avoid affecting far-stage behavior

intended_fix: condition reward by progress or stability stage
expected_effect: Handle stage-dependent task conflicts and discourage low-progress survival.
risk: Stage gates may be imperfect before trajectory memory is available.
